title: Two-point linear interpolation is affine interpolation in normalized coordinates

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 8 (Polynomial Interpolation).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: On a nondegenerate interval, the two-point linear interpolant is the affine interpolant with normalized coordinate (x-x0)/(x1-x0).
informal.expected_conclusion: Linear interpolation reduces to affine interpolation in normalized coordinates.

declarations.name: linearInterp
declarations.statement: def linearInterp (x0 x1 y0 y1 x : Real) : Real := ((x1 - x) / (x1 - x0)) * y0 + ((x - x0) / (x1 - x0)) * y1

declarations.name: affineInterp
declarations.statement: def affineInterp (theta y0 y1 : Real) : Real := (1 - theta) * y0 + theta * y1

declarations.name: linearInterp_eq_affine
declarations.statement: theorem linearInterp_eq_affine (x0 x1 y0 y1 x : Real) (h : x1 - x0 ≠ 0) : linearInterp x0 x1 y0 y1 x = affineInterp ((x - x0) / (x1 - x0)) y0 y1

tasks.input: Translate the interpolation theorem `linearInterp_eq_affine` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem linearInterp_eq_affine (x0 x1 y0 y1 x : Real) (h : x1 - x0 ≠ 0) : linearInterp x0 x1 y0 y1 x = affineInterp ((x - x0) / (x1 - x0)) y0 y1

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the interpolation proof.
